title: Signal Dry Bulk Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week : Dry Bulk Grain Flows from Brazil to China
Monthly tonnes of grain from Brazil to China with Panamax vessel class surpassed volumes of the previous years
The last week of October witnessed a downward trend in the Capesize freight market momentum, while the smaller vessel segments experienced a relative flat momentum. Meanwhile, the grain flows from Brazil to China with the Panamax vessel class recorded a significant surge in October that supported a firmer momentum for Panamax vessel freight rates (as envisaged in the image above). Amid the weaker picture of the Capesize vessel freight rates, there is a positive side on the Chinese economy.

children.title: Chart of the Week : Dry Bulk Grain Flows from Brazil to China
section_path: Chart of the Week : Dry Bulk Grain Flows from Brazil to China
children.section_path_text: Chart of the Week : Dry Bulk Grain Flows from Brazil to China
children.summary:
Monthly tonnes of grain from Brazil to China with Panamax vessel class surpassed volumes of the previous years
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-25T135854.159.png | assets/2023-10-25_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-25t135854159_c70997c2a0c2.png
The last week of October witnessed a downward trend in the Capesize freight market momentum, while the smaller vessel segments experienced a relative flat momentum. Meanwhile, the grain flows from Brazil to China with the Panamax vessel class recorded a significant surge in October that supported a firmer momentum for Panamax vessel freight rates (as envisaged in the image above).

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT
children.summary:
‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes Market Rates ($/t) Weaker
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-25T140018.973.png | assets/2023-10-25_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-25t140018973_c70997c2a0c2.png
The last week of October saw a weaker momentum following the notable increase in Capesize rates for the Brazil to North China route, while there seemed to be a flat momentum for the other vessel size segments. Capesize vessel freight rates from Brazil to North China fell to $23/tonne, a 26% monthly increase.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY
children.summary:
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas Ballasters (#vessels) Mixed
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-25T140018.973.png | assets/2023-10-25_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-25t140018973_c70997c2a0c2.png
In terms of the number of ballast vessels, the last week of October saw an upward trend in the Capesize and Handysize ship size segments. Capesize SE Africa: The number of ballasters rose to 97, 22 higher than the low of week 40, and 4 above the annual average.

children.title: SECTION 3/ DEMAND
children.summary:
Summary of Dry Bulk Demand, per Ship Size Tonne Days Decreasing
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-25T140656.536.png | assets/2023-10-25_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-25t140656536_09e27718a718.png
Demand growth began to taper off in late October, with the exception of the Panamax segment. Capesize: After the peaks of the last three weeks, there is a downward trend in the last few days, but the growth rates are still significantly higher than at the end of week 37.

children.title: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION
children.summary:
Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports No of Vessels Increasing
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-25T140822.250.png | assets/2023-10-25_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-25t140822250_57898dea339e.png
After two weeks of stagnant activity, the level of ship congestion began to exhibit signs of increase. Capesize: The number of congested vessels increased again above 100, almost 20 vessels higher than the previous week.
